page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX-512 vector extensions and AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local data storage, the instance includes 3600 GB of NVMe SSD storage. It also features a high-speed network interface delivering 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff: while single-core performance is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, the massive core count enables strong multi-core performance. Multi-core stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and Passmark encryption tests all rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with cached small-block reads and large-block read/write operations scoring in the top tier.

The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in high-throughput operations, achieving top-tier results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. However, it is less suited for modern LLM text generation, where it registers average to poor token generation speeds. Given its high resource density, including substantial NVMe storage and 100 Gbps networking, the m5dn.24xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for distributed databases, high-concurrency web serving, and memory-intensive multi-threaded applications that can leverage its balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance designed for high-throughput workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark results indicate a tradeoff between weak single-core execution and strong multi-core performance, with the instance scoring in the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and memory bandwidth tests. It also excels in Redis SET operations and static web serving. This hardware profile makes the m5dn.24xlarge qualitatively cost-effective for high-concurrency applications, distributed databases, and network-bound workloads, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-concurrency workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth general-purpose instance combining 96 vCPUs, fast local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps networking for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM
3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for fast data access
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high throughput
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
Excellent fit for distributed databases and static web serving
Weaker single-core performance and limited LLM generation speeds
